evals.prompt: Implement the plan at .claude/plan/jwt-auth.md — it adds JWT authentication with refresh tokens to the Express API.
evals.expected_output: Claude reads the plan, implements the changes, calls mcp__codex__codex for review with structured verdict prompt, fixes CRITICAL/HIGH issues, and delivers a summary table.
expectations: Claude reads the plan file before implementing (Read tool called on .claude/plan/jwt-auth.md)
Claude uses Edit or Write tools to modify production files
Claude calls mcp__codex__codex (not Bash codex review) for the review phase
The Codex prompt includes 'VERDICT: APPROVED | WARNING | BLOCKED' format
The Codex call includes developer-instructions parameter with 'Be concise'
Claude outputs a delivery table with File, Operation, and Description columns
Claude reports the Codex review result (APPROVED or iteration count) in delivery

evals.prompt: Add input validation to the registration endpoint — validate email format, password strength, and username length.
evals.expected_output: Claude implements directly (inline task, no plan file), calls Codex for review, and delivers.
expectations: Claude does NOT attempt to read a plan file (uses task description directly)
Claude calls mcp__codex__codex for review after implementation
The Codex prompt includes 'git diff HEAD' instruction
If Codex returns WARNING or BLOCKED, Claude fixes CRITICAL/HIGH issues before re-reviewing
If MEDIUM/LOW issues remain after APPROVED, Claude asks user whether to fix them
Delivery includes a changes table and review result

evals.prompt: Add a caching layer to the user service, session service, product service, order service, and notification service.
evals.expected_output: Claude dispatches subagents for the 5+ tasks (3+ threshold) rather than implementing inline, then calls Codex for review.
expectations: Claude identifies this as 3+ implementation tasks and dispatches Task subagents
Claude records git SHA before each subagent dispatch (git rev-parse HEAD)
Claude runs git diff after each subagent to verify changes
Claude runs scoped lint/tests after each subagent completes
Claude calls mcp__codex__codex for review after all subagents complete
Claude does NOT use Bash codex review command

evals.prompt: Add a health check endpoint to the API server.
evals.expected_output: Claude detects that mcp__codex__codex is not available and stops with a clear error message — does NOT proceed without review.
expectations: Claude checks for mcp__codex__codex availability before Phase 3
Claude stops and tells the user that Codex MCP is not available
Claude mentions that this skill requires Codex for review
Claude does NOT deliver a completion table without Codex review
Claude does NOT fall back to a non-Codex review — it fails closed
